steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
The first part is: “The Argonautica, an epic poem of Greek origin, dates back to the 3rd century BCE” — an independent clause.

The second part is: “the poem's heroic narrative still captivates modern readers” — also an independent clause.

These two independent clauses need to be properly connected.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A comma + coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses.

Choice C correctly uses this structure, placing comma + yet between the clauses to express the contrast between the poem's ancient origin and its continuing modern appeal.

The other options either omit the needed conjunction (A), position the conjunction incorrectly (B), or inappropriately separate the clauses with a period when they should be unified (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
